baseline.reason: Baseline claims the root cause is a stride/linesize-vs-width mismatch, but the actual fix adds bounds checks (if w>1, w>2, w>3) for small widths. No linesize mismatch exists in the vulnerability — the OOB is simply writing dst[1] when plane->width <= 1. The entire stride-mismatch theory is unsupported speculation.

vastdb.reason: Correctly identifies the root cause: plane->width becomes 0 or 1 for small inputs after chroma subsampling, and gaussian_blur unconditionally writes to dst[1] without checking if width is large enough. The output is truncated (cuts off mid-sentence before covering dst[i] and dst[i+1] post-loop accesses), but its core analysis is accurate.
